nodes.text: The jukebox is a Wurlitzer of the wrong vintage to be in a diner this size. The selection wheel sticks at K and again at S. The card behind the glass has been hand-typed on the same typewriter as the menu and has been corrected, in pencil, more than once.
A-04 — Burzum — Det Som Engang Var. Marked WAGNER'S, underlined twice.
B-11 — Faust (the band, the Krautrock one) — The Faust Tapes, side B. The label says: not the regular.
C-07 — John Cale — Paris 1919. Pencilled note: ask the owner before playing C-07.
D-02 — A song listed only as SHILLELAGH. No artist. No B-side. The card says, in someone's handwriting: Plays once a day. Has been playing for longer than the jukebox.
E-09 — Patsy Cline — Crazy. Three pencilled crosses beside the title; you cannot tell whether they are marking the song as favored or warning you off.
You put a finger to the glass. The selection wheel is warm. There is no coin slot.
